fragility_points: Round-number rejection or short-term reversal could prevent the touch.
A stricter-than-assumed settlement methodology could lower true hit probability.
key_assumptions: A qualifying result is based on an intraperiod hit/touch rather than a close.
The official settlement source will behave similarly to checked market-data sources.
No sharp downside interruption prevents a brief wick above $2,400.
main_logical_chain: Independent price verification showed ETH near but still below $2,400.
Because this is a short-window path event, near-threshold status is bullish but not equivalent to settlement.
Therefore yes remains the base case, but probability should be discounted below the market.
main_thesis: ETH likely reaches $2,400 during April 13-19, but the market is modestly overconfident because the threshold had not yet been printed in checked independent sources and short-window path risk remains real.
source_quality_view: Primary market surface plus two independent contextual price checks gave adequate low-difficulty coverage, but full settlement-text verification remained slightly incomplete.
strongest_disconfirmers: CoinGecko and Binance checks did not show a verified 2400+ print yet.
The exact settlement-source mechanics were not fully visible in the fetched public rules extract.
strongest_supports: ETH was already trading just below the threshold, making a qualifying touch plausible within the window.
Independent checks placed ETH in the low-to-mid 2390s, so only a small move was needed.
timing_relevance: High; this is a date-bounded threshold-hit market where path and wick timing matter more than medium-term ETH direction.
unresolved_ambiguities: Full exact Polymarket rules wording/source was not captured in the extracted page text.
what_would_change_view: A verified official-source print above $2,400 would move the estimate toward the market; repeated rejection below 2400 or stricter rule wording would move it lower.
